metadata.description: HTTP 方法: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH/HEAD/OPTIONS. These are HTTP methods used in web development to interact with resources on a server. Here's a brief explanation of each: - **GET**: Requests a representation of the specified resource. - **POST**: Submits an entity to the specified resource, often for handling forms or creating new resources. - **PUT**: Replaces all current representations of the target resource with the request payload. - **DELETE**: Deletes the specified resource. - **PATCH**: Applies partial modifications to a resource. - **HEAD**: Similar to GET but without the response body; used to check if a resource exists and get its metadata. - **OPTIONS**: Returns the HTTP methods that the server supports for the specified URL. Here's a Python code example using Flask, a popular web framework in Python: ```python from flask import Flask, request app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/example', methods=['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS']) def handle_request(): if request.method == 'GET': return "This is a GET request" elif request.method == 'POST': # Handle POST data pass elif request.method == 'PUT': # Handle PUT data pass elif request.method == 'DELETE': # Handle DELETE operation pass elif request.method == 'PATCH': # Handle PATCH data pass elif request.method == 'HEAD': return "", 200, {'Content-Length': '0'} elif request.method == 'OPTIONS': # Return allowed methods return '', 200, { 'Allow': ', '.join(['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS']) } if __name__ == '__main__': app.run(debug=True) ``` This example sets up a Flask application that can handle all the mentioned HTTP methods.

data_pointers.title: HTTP 方法
data_pointers.content_preview: HTTP 方法: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH/HEAD/OPTIONS. These are HTTP methods used in web development to interact with resources on a server. Here's a brief explanation of each: - **GET**: Requests a representation of the specified resource. - **POST**: Submits an entity to the specified resource, often for handling forms or creating new resources. - **PUT**: Replaces all current representations of the target resource with the request payload. - **DELETE**: Deletes the specified resource. - **PATCH**: Applies partial modifications to a resource. - **HEAD**: Similar to GET but without the response body; used to check if a resource exists and get its metadata. - **OPTIONS**: Returns the HTTP methods that the server supports for the specified URL. Here's a Python code example using Flask, a popular web framework in Python: ```python from flask import Flask, request app = Flask(__name__) @app.route('/example', methods=['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS']) def handle_request(): if request.method == 'GET': return "This is a GET request" elif request.method == 'POST': # Handle POST data pass elif request.method == 'PUT': # Handle PUT data pass elif request.method == 'DELETE': # Handle DELETE operation pass elif request.method == 'PATCH': # Handle PATCH data pass elif request.method == 'HEAD': return "", 200, {'Content-Length': '0'} elif request.method == 'OPTIONS': # Return allowed methods return '', 200, { 'Allow': ', '.join(['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'DELETE', 'PATCH', 'HEAD', 'OPTIONS']) } if __name__ == '__main__': app.run(debug=True) ``` This example sets up a Flask application that can handle all the mentioned HTTP methods.

content.full:
## 定义
HTTP 方法是客户端向服务器发送请求时指定的操作类型，用于指示服务器对资源执行何种动作。每个 HTTP 请求都包含一个方法，常见的有 GET、POST、PUT、DELETE 等，分别对应资源的读取、创建、更新和删除操作。

## 核心要点
- GET 方法用于请求获取指定资源，是幂等的且不应有副作用，通常用于数据查询。
- POST 方法用于向服务器提交数据，可能创建新资源或触发处理，非幂等且可能多次提交产生不同结果。
- PUT 方法用于上传或替换指定资源，是幂等的，多次请求结果与一次相同。
- DELETE 方法用于删除指定资源，也是幂等的，但实际删除行为可能受服务器策略影响。
- 其他方法如 HEAD、OPTIONS、PATCH 等提供额外功能：HEAD 类似 GET 但只返回头部，OPTIONS 用于查询支持的方法，PATCH 用于部分更新资源。

## 应用场景
在 Web 开发中，GET 常用于获取页面或 API 数据，POST 用于表单提交或创建新记录，PUT 用于更新整个资源（如修改用户信息），DELETE 用于删除资源（如移除文章）。RESTful API 设计严格遵循这些方法语义，以实现统一接口和可预测行为。

## 相关概念
HTTP 方法与状态码紧密相关，例如 200 OK 常伴随 GET 成功，201 Created 用于 POST 创建资源，204 No Content 用于 DELETE 成功。此外，安全方法（如 GET、HEAD）不改变服务器状态，幂等方法（如 GET、PUT、DELETE）保证多次请求效果一致，这些特性影响缓存和重试机制。
